name: Firefighters & Company
description_short: Firefighters & Company Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, loans, and investment services primarily to firefighters and related groups in the Dayton, Ohio area.
description_long: Firefighters & Company Federal Credit Union operates as a federally insured credit union serving firefighters, emergency personnel, and affiliated members in the Dayton region. The institution was established to provide financial services tailored to first responders and their families, with membership tied to specific employer groups and professional affiliations. The credit union maintains a significant physical presence with over 40 branches in the Dayton area and access to more than 5,000 shared branching locations nationwide through cooperative agreements. The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including high-yield checking accounts (Kasasa Cash earning up to 5.0% APY), savings accounts, share certificates with competitive rates (up to 3.85% APY on 48-60 month terms), IRAs, vehicle loans with an Auto Link buying service, mortgages, personal loans, VISA credit products, student loans, and Health Savings Accounts. They provide digital banking through online and mobile platforms with bill pay, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. Additional services include shared branching access, ATM locating, financial counseling, and insurance options including A&D coverage and auto/home insurance partnerships. What distinguishes this credit union is its specialized focus on the firefighter community and its aggressive deposit rates—the Kasasa Cash checking account offering 5.0% APY up to $25,000 represents an exceptional rate in the current market for reward checking products. The credit union also emphasizes member education through their Dispatch newsletter covering financial topics, provides coin counting machines, discount tickets, and maintains dedicated enrollment programs for specific employer groups like the City of Dayton. Their Auto Link service specifically addresses vehicle purchasing, a common financial need for this demographic. However, membership eligibility appears restricted to specific occupational and organizational groups rather than being open to the general public, which limits accessibility. While the website displays current rates and products, details about APR ranges, loan approval criteria, membership fees, and specific eligibility requirements are not fully transparent on the public-facing pages. The credit union's strength in rates and member services is offset by geographic concentration in the Dayton market despite nationwide branching access.
services: Kasasa Cash checking accounts with up to 5.0% APY
Kasasa Cash Back checking with 4.0% cash back on debit purchases
Share Certificates (CDs) with terms from 6 to 60 months
Vehicle loans with Auto Link buying service
Personal loans and Share Secured loans
VISA credit cards and debit cards (MasterMoney)
Online banking, mobile banking, and bill pay
pros: Kasasa Cash checking account earns up to 5.0% APY on balances up to $25,000—significantly above national checking averages
Share Certificate (CD) rates competitive at 3.85% APY on 48-60 month terms as of website publication
Access to 5,000+ shared branches nationwide plus 40+ Dayton-area locations for in-person services
Specialized Auto Link vehicle buying service designed for consumer auto purchasing
Multiple checking options including Kasasa Cash Back offering 4.0% cash back on debit purchases
Health Savings Accounts and dedicated HSA enrollment support (including City of Dayton program)
Free financial counseling and financial planning services available to members
cons: Membership appears restricted to firefighters, emergency personnel, and affiliated organizational groups—not open to general public
Website lacks transparency on APR ranges for loans, membership fees, and specific eligibility criteria
Primary service area concentration in Dayton, Ohio despite nationwide shared branching access
Kasasa reward checking account requires monthly qualifications (12 debit purchases, e-statements enrollment, monthly login) to earn advertised rates
Limited information available about business loan or commercial services on public website
best_for: Firefighters and emergency personnel in the Dayton area seeking competitive deposit rates and specialized financial services
Members of eligible employer groups or organizations affiliated with Firefighters & Company Federal Credit Union
Consumers prioritizing high-yield checking accounts and willing to meet monthly activity requirements for rewards
Vehicle buyers seeking guidance through the Auto Link auto buying service and competitive auto loan rates
diagnosis: Firefighters & Company Federal Credit Union is best for firefighters, emergency personnel, and members of affiliated organizations in the Dayton area seeking competitive deposit rates—particularly the 5.0% APY checking account—and comprehensive consumer financial services. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted to specific occupational and organizational groups rather than being available to the general public, and the institution's main service concentration is in the Ohio region despite nationwide shared branching access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
